author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
On the words of the Gospel, Luke xi. 5, ‘Which of you shall have a friend, and shall go unto him at midnight,’ etc.

ccel.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

title: On the words of the Gospel, Luke xi. 5, ‘Which of you shall have a friend, and shall go unto him at midnight,’ etc.

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: We have heard our Lord, the Heavenly Master, and most faithful Counsellor exhorting us, who at once exhorteth us to ask, and giveth when we ask.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: We have heard Him in the Gospel exhorting us to ask instantly, and to knock even after the likeness of intrusive importunity.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For He has set before us, for the sake of example, “If any of you had a friend, and were to ask of him at night for three loaves, when a friend out of his way had come to him, and he had nothing to set before him; and he were to answer that he was now at rest, and his servants with him, and that he must not be disturbed by his entreaties; but the other were to be instant and persevering in knocking, and not being alarmed in modesty to depart, but compelled by necessity to continue on; that he would rise, though not for friendship’s sake, at least for the other’s importunity, and would give him as many as he wished.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And how many did he wish?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He wished for no more than three.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: To this parable then, the Lord adjoined an exhortation, and urged us earnestly to ask, seek, knock, till we receive what we ask, and seek, and knock for, making use of an example from a contrary case; as of that “judge who neither feared God, nor regarded man,” and yet when a certain widow besought him day by day, overcome by her importunity, he gave her that which he could not in kindness give her, against his will.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But our Lord Jesus Christ, who is in the midst of us a Petitioner, with God a Giver, would not surely exhort us so strongly to ask, if He were not willing to give.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let then the slothfulness of men be put to shame; He is more willing to give, than we to receive; He is more willing to show mercy, than we to be delivered from misery; and doubtless if we shall not be delivered, we shall abide in misery.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For the exhortation He giveth us, He giveth only for our own sakes.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let us awake, and believe Him who exhorteth us, obey Him who promiseth us, and rejoice in Him who giveth unto us.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For peradventure, some time or other some friend out of his way has come to us too, and we have found nothing to set before him; and under the experience of this necessity, we have received both for ourselves and him.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For it cannot be, but that some one of us hath fallen in with a friend who asked him something, which he could not answer; and then he has discovered that he has it not, when he is pressed to give it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: A friend has come to thee “out of the way,” out, that is, of the life of this world, in which all men are passing along as strangers, and no one abides here as possessor; but to every man it is said, “Thou hast been refreshed, pass on, go on thy way, give place to the next comer.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Or perhaps from an evil “way,” that is, from an evil life, some friend of thine wearied out, and not finding the truth, by the hearing and perceiving of which he may be made happy, but exhausted amid all the lust and poverty of the world, comes to thee, as to a Christian, and says, “Give me an account of this, make me a Christian.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And he asks what it may be thou didst not know through the simplicity of thy faith; and so thou hast not whereby to recruit him in his hunger, and reminded thus thou discoverest thine own indigence; and when thou wishest to teach thou art forced to learn; and whilst thou dost blush before him who asked thee, as not finding in thyself what he was seeking for, thou art compelled to seek, that thou mayest be thought worthy to find.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And where shouldest thou seek.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Where but in the books of the Lord?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Peradventure what he has asked is contained in the book, but it is obscure.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Perhaps the Apostle has declared it in some Epistle: declared it in such wise, that thou canst read, but canst not understand it: thou art not permitted to pass on.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For the interrogator urges thee; Paul himself, or Peter, or any of the Prophets thou art not allowed to ask.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For this family is now at rest with their Lord, and intense is the ignorance of this life, that is, it is midnight, and thy hungry friend is urgent upon thee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: A simple faith haply sufficed thee, him it suffices not.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Is he then to be abandoned?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Is he to be cast out of thy house?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Therefore unto the Lord Himself, unto Him with whom the family is at rest, knock by prayer, ask, be instant.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He will not, as that friend in the parable, arise and give thee as overcome by importunity.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He wisheth to give; thou for thy knocking hast not yet received; knock on; He wisheth to give.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And what He wisheth to give, He deferreth, that thou mayest long the more for it when deferred, lest if given quickly it should be lightly esteemed.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But when thou hast gotten the three loaves, that is, to feed on and understand the Trinity, thou hast that whereby thou mayest both live thyself, and feed others.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now thou needest not fear the stranger who comes out of his way to thee, but by taking him in mayest make him a citizen of the household: nor needest thou fear lest thou come to the end of it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: That Bread will not come to an end, but it will put an end to thine indigence.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It is Bread, God the Father, and it is Bread, God the Son, and it is Bread, God the Holy Ghost.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Father Eternal, the Son Coeternal with Him, and the Holy Ghost Coeternal.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Father Unchangeable, the Son Unchangeable, the Holy Ghost Unchangeable.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Father Creator, and the Son, and the Holy Ghost.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Father the Shepherd and the Giver of life, and the Son, and the Holy Ghost.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Father the Food and Bread eternal, and the Son, and the Holy Ghost.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Learn, and teach; live thyself, and feed others.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: God who giveth to thee, giveth thee nothing better than Himself.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: O thou greedy one, what else wast thou seeking for?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Or if thou seek for aught else, what will suffice thee whom God doth suffice not?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But necessary it is that thou have charity, that thou have faith, that thou have hope; that that which is given may be sweet unto thee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And these same, faith, hope, charity, are three.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And these too are gifts of God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For faith we have received from Him; “As God,” saith he, “hath distributed to every one the measure of faith.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And hope we have received from Him, to whom it is said, “Wherein Thou hast caused me to hope.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And charity we have received from Him, of whom it is said, “The charity of God is shed abroad in our hearts by the Holy Ghost, which hath been given to us.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now these three are likewise in some measure different; but all gifts of God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For “there abide these three, faith, hope, charity; but the greatest of these is charity.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: In those loaves it is not said that any one loaf was greater than the others; but simply that three loaves were asked for, and were given.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: See other three things: “Who is there of you, whom if his son ask a loaf, will he give him a stone?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Or who is there of you of whom if his son ask a fish, will he give him a serpent? or if he ask an egg, will he offer him a scorpion?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If ye then, being evil, know how to give good gifts unto your children, how much more shall your Father which is in heaven give good things to them that ask him!”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let us then again consider these three things, if haply there be not here those three, “faith, hope, charity; but the greatest of these is charity.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Set down then these three things, a loaf, a fish, an egg; the greatest of these is a loaf.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Therefore in these three things do we well understand charity by “the loaf.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: On which account He has opposed a stone to a loaf; because hardness is contrary to charity.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: By “a fish” we understand faith.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: A certain holy man has said, and we are glad to say it too; “The ‘good fish’ is a godly faith.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It lives amidst the waves, and is not broken or dissolved by the waves.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Amidst the temptations and tempests of this world, liveth godly faith; the world rages, yet it is uninjured.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Observe only that serpent is contrary to faith.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For My faith is she betrothed to whom it is said in the Song of Songs, “Come from Lebanon, My spouse, coming and passing over to Me from the beginning of faith.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Therefore betrothed too, because faith is the beginning of betrothal.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For something is promised by the bridegroom, and by this plighted faith is he held bound.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now to the fish the Lord opposed the serpent, to faith the devil.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Wherefore to this betrothed one does the Apostle say, “I have betrothed you to One Husband, to present you a chaste virgin to Christ.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And, “I fear lest as the serpent beguiled Eve through his subtilty, so your minds also should be corrupted from the purity which is in Christ;” that is, which is in the faith of Christ.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For he says, “That Christ may dwell in your hearts by faith.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Therefore let not the devil corrupt our faith, let him not devour the fish.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: There remains hope, which, as I think, is compared to an egg.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For hope has not yet arrived at attainment; and an egg is something, but not yet the chicken.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So then quadrupeds give birth to young ones, but birds to the hope of young.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Hope therefore exhorts us to this, to despise things present, to wait for things to come; “forgetting those things which are behind,” let us, with the Apostle,” reach forth unto those things which are before.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For so he says; “But one thing I do, forgetting those things which are behind, reaching forth unto those things which are before, I follow on earnestly unto the prize of the high calling of God in Christ Jesus.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Nothing then is so hostile to hope, as to “look back,” to place hope, that is, in those things which flit by and pass away; but in those things should we place it, which are not yet given, but which sometime will be given, and will never pass away.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But when the world is deluged by trials, as it were the sulphureous rain of Sodom, the example of Lot’s wife must be feared.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For she “looked behind;” and in the spot where she looked behind, there did she remain.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: She was turned into salt, that she might season the wise by her example.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Of this hope the Apostle Paul speaketh thus; “For we are saved in hope; but hope that is seen is not hope; for what a man seeth, why doth he yet hope for: but if we hope for that we see not, then do we with patience wait for it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For what a man seeth, why doth he yet hope for.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It is an egg, and not as yet the chicken.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And it is covered with a shell; it is not seen because it is covered; let it be with patience waited for; let it feel the warmth, that it may come to life.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Press on, “reach forth unto the things which are before, forget the past.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For the things which are seen, are temporal.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Not looking back,” says he, “at the things which are seen, but at the things which are not seen.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For the things which are seen are temporal; but the things which are not seen are eternal.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Unto those things which are not seen then extend thy hope, wait, endure.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Look not back.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Fear “the scorpion” for thine “egg.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: See how he wounds with the tail, which he has behind him.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let not then the “scorpion” crush thine “egg,” let not this world crush thy hope (so to say) with its poison, therefore against thee, because behind.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: How loudly does the world talk to thee, what an uproar does it make behind thy back, that thou mayest look back! that is, that thou mayest place thy hope in present things (and yet not even present, for they cannot be called present which have no fixedness), and mayest turn thy mind away from that which Christ hath promised, and not yet given, but who, seeing He is faithful, will give it, and mayest be content to look for rest in a perishing world.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For this cause does God mingle bitternesses with the felicities of earth, that another felicity may be sought, in whose sweetness there is no deceit; yet by these very bitternesses does the world endeavour to turn thee away from thy longing pursuit after the things “which are before,” and to turn thee back.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For these bitternesses, for these tribulations dost thou murmur and say, “See, all things are perishing in Christian times.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What complaint is this!

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: God hath not promised me that these things shall not perish; Christ hath not promised me this.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Eternal hath promised things eternal: if I believe, from a mortal, I shall be made eternal.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What noise is this, O world impure! what murmuring is this!

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Why art thou trying to turn me back?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Perishing as thou art, thou wishest to detain me; what wouldest thou do, if thou hadst any permanence?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Whom wouldest thou not beguile by thy sweetness, if with all thy bitternesses thou dost impose thy false nourishment upon us?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For me, if I have hope, if I hold fast my hope, my “egg” has not been wounded by the “scorpion.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “I will bless the Lord at all times, His praise shall be ever in my mouth.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Be the world prosperous, or be the world turned upside down; “I will bless the Lord,” who made the world.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Yes, verily, I will bless Him.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Be it well with me according to the flesh, or be it ill according to the flesh, “I will bless the Lord at all times, His praise shall be ever in my mouth.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For if I bless when it is well, and blaspheme when it is ill with me; I have received the “scorpion’s” sting, being pricked “I have looked back;” which be far from us.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “The Lord gave, the Lord hath taken away: it is done, as the Lord pleased; blessed be the name of the Lord.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The city which has given us birth according to the flesh still abideth, God be thanked.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: O that it may receive a spiritual birth, and together with us pass over unto eternity!

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If the city which has given us birth according to the flesh abide not, yet that which has given us birth according to the Spirit abides for ever.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “The Lord doth build up Jerusalem.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Has He by sleeping brought His building to ruin, or by not keeping it, let the enemy into it?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Except the Lord keep the city, he that keepeth it waketh but in vain.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And what “city”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “He that keepeth Israel shall neither slumber nor sleep.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is Israel, but the seed of Abraham?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What the seed of Abraham, but Christ?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “And to thy seed,” he says, “which is Christ.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And to us what says he?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “But ye are Christ’s, therefore Abraham’s seed, heirs according to the promise.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “In thy seed,” saith He, “shall all nations be blessed.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The holy city, the faithful city, the city on earth a sojourner, hath its foundation in heaven.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: O faithful one, do not corrupt thy hope, do not lose thy charity, “gird up thy loins,” light, and hold out thy lamps before thee; “wait for the Lord, when He will return from the wedding.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Why art thou alarmed, because the kingdoms of the earth are perishing?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Therefore hath a heavenly kingdom been promised thee, that thou mightest not perish with the kingdoms of the earth.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For it was foretold, foretold distinctly, that they should perish.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For we cannot deny that it was foretold.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thy Lord for whom thou art waiting, hath told thee, “Nation shall rise up against nation, and kingdom against kingdom.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The kingdoms of the earth have their changes; He will come of whom it is said, “and of His kingdom there shall be no end.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: They who have promised this to earthly kingdoms have not been guided by truth, but have lied through flattery.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: A certain poet of theirs has introduced Jupiter speaking, and he says of the Romans;

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: To them no bounds of empire I assign,

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Nor term of years to their immortal line.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Most certainly truth makes no such answer.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This empire which thou hast given “without term of years,” is it on earth, or in heaven?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: On earth assuredly.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And even if it were in heaven, yet “heaven and earth shall pass away.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Those things shall pass away which God hath Himself made; how much more rapidly shall that pass away which Romulus founded!

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Perhaps if we had a mind to press Virgil on this point, and tauntingly to ask him why he said it; he would take us aside privately, and say to us, “I know this as well as you, but what could I do who was selling words to the Romans, if by this kind of flattery I did not promise something which was false?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And yet even in this very instance I have been cautious, when I said, ‘I assigned to them an empire without term of years,’ I introduced their Jupiter to say it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I did not utter this falsehood in my own person, but put upon Jupiter the character of untruthfulness: as the god was false, the poet was false.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For would ye know that I well knew the truth of it?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: In another place, when I did not introduce this stone, called Jupiter, but spoke in my own person, I said,

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: ‘Th’ impending ruin of the Roman state.’

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: See how I spoke of the impending ruin of the state.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I spoke of its impending ruin.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I did not suppress it.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: When he spoke in truth he was not silent as to its ruin; when in flattery, he promised that it should abide for ever.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let us not then faint, my Brethren: an end there will be to all earthly kingdoms.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If that end be now, God knoweth.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For peradventure it is not yet, and we, through some infirmity, or mercifulness, or misery, are wishing that it may not be yet; nevertheless will it not therefore some day be?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Fix your hope in God, desire the things eternal, wait for the things eternal.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Ye are Christians, Brethren, we are all Christians.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Christ did not come down into the flesh that we might live softly; let us endure rather than love the things present; manifest is the harm of adversity, deceitful is the soft blandishment of prosperity.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Fear the sea, even when it is a calm.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: On no account let us hear in vain, “Let us lift up our hearts.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Why place we our hearts in the earth, when we see that the earth is being turned upside down?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: We cannot but exhort you, that ye may have something to say and answer in defence of your hope against the deriders and blasphemers of the Christian name.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let no one by his murmuring turn you back from waiting for the things to come.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: All who by reason of these adversities blaspheme our Christ, are the “scorpion’s” tail.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let us put our egg under the wings of that Hen of the Gospel, which crieth out to that false and abandoned city, “O Jerusalem, Jerusalem, how often would I have gathered thy children together, even as a hen her chickens, and thou wouldest not!”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let it not be said to us, “How often would I, and thou wouldest not!”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For that hen is the Divine Wisdom; but assumed flesh to accommodate Itself to its chickens.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: See the hen with feathers bristling, with wings hanging down, with voice broken, and tremulous, and faint, and languid, accommodating herself to her little ones.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Our egg then, that is, our hope, let us place beneath the wings of this Hen.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Ye have noticed, it may be, how a hen will tear a scorpion in pieces.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: O then that the Hen of the Gospel would tear in pieces and devour these blasphemers, creeping out of their holes, and inflicting hurtful stings, would pass them over into Her Body, and turn them into an egg.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let them not be angry; we seem to be excited; but we do not return curses for curses.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “We are cursed, and we bless, being defamed, we entreat.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But “let him not speak of Rome, it is said of me: O that he would hold his tongue about Rome;” as though I were insulting it, and not rather entreating the Lord for it, and exhorting you all, unworthy as I am.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Be it far from me to insult it!

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Lord avert this from my heart, and from the grief of my conscience.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Have we not had many brethren there? have we not still?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Does not a large portion of the pilgrim city Jerusalem live there? has it not endured there temporal afflictions? but it has not lost the things eternal.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What can I say then, when I speak of Rome, but that is false, which they say of our Christ, that He is Rome’s destroyer, and that the gods of wood and stone were her defenders?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Add what is more costly, “gods of brass.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Add what is costlier still, “of silver and gold:” the “idols of the nations are silver and gold.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He did not say, “stone;” he did not say, “wood;” he did not say, “clay;” but, what they value highly, “silver and gold.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Yet these silver and golden idols “have eyes, and see not.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The gods of gold, of wood, are as regards their costliness unequal; but as to “having eyes, and seeing not,” they are equal.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: See to what sort of guardians learned men have entrusted Rome, to those “who have eyes, and see not.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Or if they were able to preserve Rome, why did they first perish themselves?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: They say; “Rome perished at the same time.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Nevertheless they perished.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “No,” they say, “they did not perish themselves, but their statues.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Well, how then could they keep your houses, who were not able to keep their own statues?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Alexandria once lost such gods as these.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Constantinople some time since, ever since it was made a grand city, for it was made so by a Christian Emperor, lost its false gods; and yet it has increased, and still increases, and remains.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And remain it will, as long as God pleases.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For we do not to this city either promise an eternal duration because we say this.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Carthage remains now in its possession of the Name of Christ, yet once on a time its goddess Cælestis was overthrown; because celestial she was not, but terrestrial.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And that which they say is not true, that immediately on losing her gods Rome has been taken and ruined.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It is not true at all; their images were overthrown before; and even so were the Goths with Rhadagaisus conquered.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Remember, my Brethren, remember; it is no long time since, but a few years, call it to mind.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: When all the images in the city of Rome had been overthrown, Rhadagaisus king of the Goths came with a large army, much more numerous than that of Alaric was.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Rhadagaisus was a Pagan; he sacrificed to Jupiter every day.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Everywhere it was announced, that Rhadagaisus did not cease from sacrificing.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Then said they all, “Lo, we do not sacrifice, he does sacrifice, we, who are not allowed to sacrifice must be conquered by him who does sacrifice.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But God making proof that not even temporal deliverance, nor the preservation of these earthly kingdoms, consist in these sacrifices, Rhadagaisus, by the Lord’s help, was marvellously overcome.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Afterwards came other Goths who did not sacrifice, they came, who though they were not Catholics in the Christian faith, were yet hostile and opposed to idols, and they took Rome; they conquered those who put their trust in idols, who were still seeking after the idols they had lost, and desiring still to sacrifice to the lost gods.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And amongst them too were some of our brethren, and these were afflicted also: but they had learnt to say, “I will bless the Lord at all times.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: They were involved in the afflictions of their earthly kingdom: but they lost not the kingdom of heaven; yea, rather, they were made the better for obtaining it through the exercise of tribulations.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And if they did not in their tribulations blaspheme, they came out as sound vessels from the furnace, and were filled with the blessing of the Lord.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Whereas those blasphemers, who follow and long after earthly things, who place their hope in earthly things, when these they have lost, whether they will or no, what shall they retain? where shall they abide?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Nothing without, nothing within; an empty coffer, an emptier conscience.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Where is their rest? where their salvation? where their hope?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let them then come, let them give over blaspheming, let them learn to adore; let the scorpions with their stings be devoured by the Hen, let them be turned into His body who makes them pass over into it; let them on earth be exercised, in heaven be crowned.
